column3: Validate inbound PROJ-FCR handling in DR.
column6: Confirm the primary workflow does not unintentionally process PROJ-FCR. Prepare a controlled DR-only filter for F1PPM_08-Master-Data-PROJ-FCR* and confirm DR storage is stprdasepol.
column7: Enable or manually run the DR workflow with a controlled PROJ-FCR file and monitor the run to completion.
column8: The DR workflow copies the file to techone-pol/STAGE on stprdasepol and no unintended primary-region processing occurs.
column9: Workflow run id, blob path screenshot, workflow configuration evidence.
column14: Stop and log a blocker if the workflow cannot isolate the file pattern or if storage/key vault access fails.
column15: Current inbound workflow does not explicitly match PROJ-FCR in the repo, so the exercise should treat this as a controlled DR scenario rather than assumed existing behaviour.

column3: Optionally validate outbound POL to TechOne flow in DR.
column6: Confirm exercise scope includes outbound validation and identify a safe export file in pol-techone/EXPORTS.
column7: Run or simulate the DR outbound workflow and monitor upload, archive, and delete behaviour.
column8: The DR workflow reads from pol-techone/EXPORTS on stprdasepol, archives the file, uploads it to TechOne SFTP, and removes the source blob.
column9: Workflow run id, SFTP confirmation, archive blob evidence.
column14: Defer if outbound validation is out of scope or production-connected risk is too high.
column15: Keep this optional for the first tabletop unless outbound confirmation is a business requirement.

column3: Validate FCR import execution in DR.
column6: Confirm linked services test successfully in DR. Confirm source file is present in techone-pol/STAGE on stprdasepol and decide whether manual execution or isolated trigger validation will be used.
column7: Run FCRIMPORT in adf-prd-ae-ais and monitor copy, upsert, and delete activities.
column8: The pipeline imports PROJ-FCR data into Altus and deletes the processed source file according to pipeline logic.
column9: Pipeline run id, linked service test results, target entity validation, source file deletion evidence.
column14: Stop if linked service, function key, key vault, or source storage access fails.
column15: Prefer manual pipeline execution for tabletop/QFDR because the shared 2_am_trigger contains multiple pipelines.

column6: Confirm DR Function App appsettings use stprdasepol and a staged file exists under techone-pol/STAGE.
column7: Execute TECHONE_FILES_ARCHIVE from adf-prd-ae-ais and monitor the linked Azure Function activity TechOneFileArchiveProcessor.
column8: The DR function copies files from techone-pol/STAGE into the archive container while preserving the blob path.
column9: Pipeline run id, Function App log entry, archive container screenshot.
column14: Stop and raise a blocker if the DR Function App still points at the wrong storage account or cannot access archive.
column15: This validates both Function App storage access and the ADF to Function App linked-service path.

column3: Validate Inflo workspace update path through DR Function App and APIM.
column6: Confirm kv-prd-ae-pol contains apimSubscriptionKey and the ADF polAzureFunction linked service resolves the host function key. Place a controlled OCPPM_18-Projects-* file in inflo-pol/IMPORTS.
column7: Execute WORKSPACE in adf-prd-ae-ais and monitor the InfloProjectUpdate function through completion.
column8: The DR function reads the import file from inflo-pol/IMPORTS, calls Altus through APIM, and updates the workspace URL where expected.
column9: Pipeline run id, Function App log, Altus evidence, APIM/key vault validation.
column14: Stop if key vault secret resolution or APIM connectivity fails.
column15: APIM remains ASE-hosted in the current design; this scenario validates dependency reachability, not APIM failover.

column3: Validate DR secret resolution for ADF

column9: Test secret resolution through DR ADF linked services and DR application runtime dependencies.
column10: DR ADF, Function App, and Logic App can resolve the secrets required for the exercise without falling back to primary-region vault references.
